<!-- Copilot mirror of commands/ticket-ship.md. Generated by bin/sync-copilot-prompts.
     Edit the Claude source, not this file — regenerate instead. -->

> **Plane MCP pre-flight (Copilot — check this before the Plane path steps below):**
>
> Scan your available tool list for at least one tool matching `mcp__plane__*`, `plane_*`, or `mcp_plane_*`.
> - **None found → STOP.** Print: "Error: Plane MCP tools are not accessible. In VS Code, open the MCP panel (View → MCP Servers), ensure the `plane` server shows as Connected, and retry. Do not fall back to creating markdown tickets." Do not continue to the Markdown path.
> - **Found → note the active prefix** (e.g. `mcp__plane__` or `plane_`) and use it for every tool call in this session.
>
> **If `.claude/plane-config.md` exists, the Markdown path is off-limits** regardless of MCP status — never silently degrade to local TKT-*.md files when the project uses Plane.


# Ship a Ticket

You are merging an approved ticket to main and (optionally) deploying.

## Pre-flight: detect backend

- If `.claude/plane-config.md` exists → **Plane path**.
- Else if `.claude/ticket-config.md` + `tickets/` → **Markdown path**.
- Else: error, tell the user to run `/ticket-install`.

## Input (both backends)

- Argument `{ID}` (or bare number; resolves per project identifier / ticket prefix + padding).
- Optional `--target <branch>` — overrides the Main branch from `ticket-config.md`. Useful when a feature branch was cut from something other than the project's canonical trunk (e.g. a Plan 2 migration branch).


## Plane path

### Pre-flight

1. Load `.claude/plane-config.md` + `.claude/ticket-config.md`. From the latter, read Test / Build / Deploy commands and Main branch (or use `--target`, or `git symbolic-ref refs/remotes/origin/HEAD`, or `main`).
2. Retrieve the work item: `mcp__plane__retrieve_work_item_by_identifier`. 404 → error.
3. **State gate:**
   - Terminal (`Done` / `Cancelled`) → STOP with `/ticket-reopen` hint.
   - Must be `In Review`. Any other state → error.
4. Verify we're on the feature branch (`ticket/<lowercased-id>-<slug>`).
5. Clean working tree required.

### Phase 1: Final Regression Test (rebase-inclusive)

1. **Fetch target:** `git fetch origin {target}` (best-effort; continue if no remote).
2. **Rebase onto target:**
   - If a remote exists: `git rebase origin/{target}`.
   - Else: `git rebase {target}`.
   - If conflicts arise, STOP and report. Do NOT auto-resolve.
3. **Tests** (if configured): all must pass post-rebase. Fail → STOP.
4. **Build** (if configured): clean. Fail → STOP.

### Phase 2: Regression Report → Plane

Read current `description_html`, then `update_work_item` to append:

```html
<h3>Regression Report</h3>
<ul>
  <li>Rebase onto <code>{target}</code>: clean</li>
  <li>Tests after rebase: {N} passing, 0 failing (or <em>skipped — no Test configured</em>)</li>
  <li>Build after rebase: clean (or <em>skipped</em>)</li>
  <li>Tested at: {timestamp}</li>
</ul>
```

Read-modify-write; don't truncate.

### Phase 3: Merge

1. `git checkout {target}`
2. `git pull origin {target}` (best-effort)
3. `git merge ticket/{branch} --no-ff -m "Merge {ID}: {title}"` — merge commit message must include the Plane identifier.
4. Tests one more time on the merged target. Failure → `git reset --hard HEAD~1`, report, STOP. No broken commits on target.
5. Build one more time. Same.

### Phase 4: Deploy (only if Deploy is configured)

1. `git push origin {target}`.
2. Run the Deploy command from `ticket-config.md`.
3. Wait for completion; capture status.

If Deploy is empty or `(none)`:

1. `git push origin {target}` (skip if no remote).
2. Note in output that no deploy was configured.

### Phase 5: Attach PR link + transition state

1. If a PR URL is known (derive from git remote + target compare URL, or skip if unavailable): `mcp__plane__create_work_item_link(work_item_id, url=<PR URL>)`.
2. Transition state to `Done`: `mcp__plane__update_work_item(state=<done state id>)`.

### Phase 6: Cleanup (no archive — state is the archive)

Plane has no shipped/ directory; state `Done` IS the archive. Skip the markdown-path Phase 6.

1. Delete the feature branch locally: `git branch -d ticket/{lowercased-id}-{slug}`.
2. Delete on remote if it was pushed: `git push origin --delete ticket/{lowercased-id}-{slug}` (best-effort).

### Phase 7: Decruft (automatic)

Unchanged from the markdown path — worktrees and preview processes are execution-layer (Q13) and live in local filesystem, not Plane.

1. Kill preview components for this ticket (read `.worktrees/ticket-{lowercased-id}/.preview.pid`; SIGTERM → SIGKILL in reverse launch order; skip `-` PIDs). Remove `.preview.pid` / `.preview.meta`.
2. `git worktree remove .worktrees/ticket-{lowercased-id}` (fallback to `--force` then `rm -rf + git worktree prune`).
3. Rollup preview rebuild: if a `.worktrees/batch-preview-*/` worktree has a live `.preview.pid`, kill it, remove the scratch branch, recreate from `{target}` with all remaining In-Review work items' branches merged in identifier order, relaunch. If no In-Review items remain, kill the rollup and don't rebuild.
4. Report decruft actions in the final output.

### Finish

```
{ID} SHIPPED

Merged to {target}: {merge commit hash}
Deployed: {timestamp or "skipped — no deploy configured"}
PR link attached: {url or "n/a"}
State: Done
Branch cleaned up: yes

Summary: {1-2 sentence description of what shipped}
```

### Rules (Plane path)

- NEVER force push to `{target}`.
- NEVER push if tests or build fail after merge.
- Reset to a safe state and report if anything goes wrong during merge/deploy.
- Merge commit message MUST reference the Plane identifier.
- Ask for confirmation before pushing to main if this is the first time using the workflow in the project.


## Markdown path

*(Unchanged. Kept in-tree until Plan 3 completes.)*

### Input (markdown)

**Locate the ticket file:** it must be at `{tickets-dir}/{ID}.md` (active set). If it lives in `shipped/`, `deferred/`, or `wontfix/`, STOP — a terminal ticket cannot be shipped again. (Use `/ticket-reopen {ID}` if this is an intentional re-ship.)

### Pre-flight Checks

- `.claude/ticket-config.md` must exist. If not, tell the user to run `/ticket-install` and stop.
- Read Test, Build, and Deploy commands from `.claude/ticket-config.md`.
- Ticket status MUST be `review` (meaning human has verified). If not, report status and stop.
- Ensure we're on the ticket's feature branch.
- Ensure working tree is clean.
- Determine the main branch: read `Main branch` from `.claude/ticket-config.md` if present. Otherwise fall back to `git symbolic-ref refs/remotes/origin/HEAD`. If the result is `master`, warn the user and suggest running `/ticket-install` to migrate.

### Phase 1: Final Regression Test

1. **Pull latest main**: `git fetch origin {main}`
2. **Rebase onto main**: `git rebase origin/{main}`
   - If conflicts arise, STOP and report them. Do NOT auto-resolve.
3. **Run Test command** from config. ALL tests must pass after rebase. If any fail, STOP and report. (Skip if no Test command configured.)
4. **Run Build command** from config. Must be clean. If errors, STOP and report. (Skip if no Build command configured.)

### Phase 2: Write Regression Report

Update the ticket's Regression Report section:
```markdown
## Regression Report
- Rebase onto {main}: clean (no conflicts)
- Tests after rebase: X passing, 0 failing (or: skipped — no test command configured)
- Build after rebase: clean (or: skipped)
- Tested at: {timestamp}
```

### Phase 3: Merge

1. Switch to main: `git checkout {main}`
2. Pull latest: `git pull origin {main}`
3. Merge the feature branch: `git merge ticket/{branch-name} --no-ff -m "Merge {TKT-ID}: {title}"`
4. Run Test command one final time on main. If ANY test fails after merge, IMMEDIATELY: `git reset --hard HEAD~1` and report. Do NOT push broken code.
5. Run Build command one final time. Same: if it fails, reset and report.

### Phase 4: Deploy (skip if no Deploy command configured)

If `.claude/ticket-config.md` has a Deploy command:
1. **Push to remote**: `git push origin {main}`
2. **Deploy**: run the Deploy command from config
3. Wait for deploy to complete and note status.

If Deploy is empty or `(none)`:
1. **Push to remote**: `git push origin {main}`
2. Skip the deploy step entirely; note in the output that no deploy is configured.

### Phase 5: Cleanup

1. Delete the feature branch: `git branch -d ticket/{branch-name}`
2. Update ticket status to `shipped`
3. Update the `updated` date

### Phase 6: Archive the ticket file

Move the ticket (and any sibling brief files) into `{tickets-dir}/shipped/` so the active set stays clean.

1. Create `{tickets-dir}/shipped/` if it does not yet exist (lazy creation — no `.gitkeep`, the first `git mv` populates it).
2. Move the ticket file with `git mv` (NOT `cp`, NOT plain `mv` — we want git to track the rename so history is preserved):
   ```
   git mv {tickets-dir}/{TKT-ID}.md {tickets-dir}/shipped/{TKT-ID}.md
   ```
3. Move any associated brief files the same way:
   ```
   git mv {tickets-dir}/{TKT-ID}.*.brief.md {tickets-dir}/shipped/
   ```
   (Skip this if no brief files exist for the ticket.)
4. Commit the move on `{main}`: `git commit -m "{TKT-ID}: archive shipped ticket"`
5. Push: `git push origin {main}`

### Phase 7: Decruft (automatic worktree + preview teardown)

This phase runs automatically — the user never asks for it. It is the mechanism that keeps the project tree clean.

1. **Kill all preview components for this ticket** (one ticket can run multiple components if it used a compound profile):
   - Read `.worktrees/ticket-{lowercased-id}/.preview.pid` if present. Each line is `{component-name}  {pid}  {port}`.
   - Kill each PID in **reverse launch order** (last-launched first — so dependents go down before the things they depend on). Skip lines where PID is `-` (command-exit components, nothing persistent to kill).
   - Use SIGTERM first, escalate to SIGKILL after 3 seconds per PID. On Windows, `taskkill /F /PID {pid}`.
   - Delete the `.preview.pid` and `.preview.meta` files.
2. **Remove the ticket's worktree** if one exists:
   - `git worktree remove .worktrees/ticket-{lowercased-id}` (with `--force` as a fallback — by ship time the worktree's contents don't matter).
   - If that fails entirely, `rm -rf .worktrees/ticket-{lowercased-id}` + `git worktree prune`.
3. **If a rollup preview is currently live** (a worktree at `.worktrees/batch-preview-*/` with a live `.preview.pid`), rebuild it:
   - Kill the current rollup preview.
   - Remove the current rollup scratch branch.
   - Recreate the rollup from `{main}` by merging every remaining `review`-status ticket branch in the batch in ID order.
   - Relaunch the preview on the new rollup.
   - If no `review`-status tickets remain, just kill the rollup and don't rebuild.
4. Report any decruft actions in the final output so the user knows what was cleaned up.

### Finish (markdown)

Output:
```
{ID} SHIPPED

Merged to {main}: {merge commit hash}
Deployed: {timestamp or "skipped — no deploy configured"}
Branch cleaned up: yes

Summary: {1-2 sentence description of what shipped}

The ticket has the full history.
```

### Rules (markdown path)

- NEVER force push to main.
- NEVER push if tests or build fail after merge.
- If anything goes wrong during merge/deploy, reset to a safe state and report.
- The merge commit message must reference the ticket ID.
- Ask for confirmation before pushing to main if this is the first time using this workflow in the project.

## Compatibility Notes

- **MCP tool mapping:** In VS Code Copilot, Plane MCP tools are typically exposed as `plane_<tool_name>` (e.g. `plane_create_work_item`), not `mcp__plane__<tool_name>` (Claude Code convention). The Plane MCP pre-flight above detects which prefix is active and uses it for the session. For explicit IDs, prefer direct identifier lookup; if unavailable, use bounded project-scoped lookup + exact match.
- **AskUserQuestion:** Copilot has no equivalent structured-question tool. When the Claude source says "use AskUserQuestion", ask the user in chat with a numbered or bulleted choice and wait for a reply.
- **Parallel subagents → sequential loop:** if the Claude source fans out subagents in parallel (e.g. ticket-investigate multi-ticket, ticket-batch), run them as a sequential loop in one agent session instead. Output and behavior are identical; only wall-clock differs.
- **Backend dispatch:** the "Pre-flight: detect backend" block is authoritative in both hosts. `.claude/plane-config.md` presence routes to the Plane path; otherwise the Markdown path runs.
